summary:
- File concerns **plans to redeem (repay/convert) the Straits Settlements Government 4½% Inscribed Stock 1935/45**, and related handling of the Straits Settlements **6% Stock 1936/51**, both originally raised in London and **re-lent to the Federated Malay States (F.M.S.)**.  
- **Geography/actors:** Straits Settlements and Federated Malay States (Malaya/Singapore/Kuala Lumpur); key institutions include the **Crown Agents for the Colonies**, the **Colonial Office** (Secretary of State Sir Philip Cunliffe-Lister; officials incl. R.V. Vernon, G.E.J. Gent), **H.M. Treasury** (A.P. Waterfield), and the **F.M.S. High Commissioner**.  
- **Key decision/timeline:** Agreement to **give notice by 15 Dec 1934** to redeem the **4½% stock at par on 15 Jun 1935** (six months’ notice required); conversion offer and press notice scheduled **7–10 Dec 1934**; conversion list to close **21 Dec 1934**.  
- **Financing proposal evolved:** initial idea of a **new loan (c. £3.6m)** to cover the gap after using the 4½% sinking fund; later shifted to a **conversion operation** into a new **F.M.S. 3% Inscribed Stock 1960/1970** (redeemable at par in 1970, with **F.M.S. option to redeem from 15 Jun 1960 on 3 months’ notice**) and **no public cash issue**, with the **Crown Agents taking up the balance using colonial funds** available for investment.  
- **Notable amounts/funds:** Straits 4½% stock outstanding **£4,200,000**; estimated sinking fund about **£600,000** by June 1935; Straits 6% stock **£5,155,000**; supplementary sinking fund cited around **£1.62m (July 1934)** and **£2.017m (Dec 1936)**; London market terms discussed included possible **3% issue around 98½** with estimated public-issue expenses **~£43,000** plus stamp duty (but these were largely avoided by the no-public-issue approach).  
- **Legislative actions:** F.M.S. passed enabling laws to borrow/repay and to secure trustee status in the UK, notably **Government Trustee Securities Enactment, 1934 (No. 21)** and **Straits Settlements Loans (Repayment) Enactment, 1934 (No. 43)**, passed by the **Federal Council in Kuala Lumpur on 26 Nov 1934**.  
- **Outcome reported:** conversion results—**£1,707,914 1s. 6d.** of Straits 4½% stock converted; remaining **£2,292,085 18s. 6d.** taken up by other colonial government funds to complete a **£4,000,000** new issue; issue expenses estimated about **£8,400** (vs. ~£43,000 if publicly issued).  
- **UK trustee listing:** Treasury issued notice (Feb 1935) adding **Federated Malay States Government 3% Inscribed Stock 1960/1970** to the list under **Section 2 of the Colonial Stock Act, 1900**, making it eligible as a trustee investment (subject to Trustee Act restrictions).
